texto: When they got to their hotel she went straight up to bed, but he paused to get a drink. There was, in the vestibule, a flower stall and he bought a handful of roses, stiffly wired into a bouquet, before proceeding to the oppressive gorgeousness of their bridal suite. The lift was lined with looking glass, so that as he shot upwards he got an endlessly duplicated version of himself, stout and nervous, a light cloak flung over his shoulder and flowers in his hand: an infinitely long row of gentlemen carrying offerings to an unforgiving past.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Margaret Kennedy sobre temas ligados a amor. A afirmação “When they got to their hotel she went straight up to bed, but he paused to get a drink. There was…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, body-image, emotion, útil em momentos de termino e emotion.
